CSS styles for a web page layout, including typography, navigation, and form elements.

# Purpose
The CSS stylesheet defines the visual presentation of an HTML document. It sets the font family for the entire document to sans-serif and applies a light gray background to the `html` element, with padding for spacing. The `body` element is constrained to a maximum width of 960 pixels, centered with automatic margins, and given a white background. Headings (`h1` to `h6`) use a serif font and a specific color, `#377ba8`, with consistent margin spacing. Links (`a`) are styled with the same color as headings. The `nav` element is styled with a light gray background and flexbox layout for alignment, including specific styles for its child elements like `h1` and `ul`. The `.content` class provides padding and styles for headers, while the `.flash` class is used for highlighted messages with a blue background and border. The `.post` class styles headers and body text, including font size adjustments and italic styling for the `.about` section. Form elements within `.content` are styled for layout and spacing, with specific styles for labels, inputs, and text areas. The `input.danger` class changes text color to red, and submit buttons are styled for alignment and minimum width.
